page: Amazon Web Services c8gn.48xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. Utilizing the ARM64 architecture, it features 192 dedicated physical cores and 192 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

In benchmark evaluations, the c8gn.48xlarge demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core CPU tests and Geekbench multi-core scores. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only large block bandwidth reaching 959.79 GB/sec and write large block bandwidth at 435.96 GB/sec, both ranking in the top 10%. Single-core performance for stress-ng and Geekbench falls within the average range. The instance also exhibits top-tier capabilities in database operations, static web serving throughput at 165.94 GB/sec, and Redis SET operations processing 381.9 million operations per second.

Network performance is a key feature, delivering a baseline bandwidth of 300 Gbps. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high physical core density and ARM64 architecture provide substantial compute resources and memory bandwidth relative to its hardware class, though the lack of local NVMe storage requires external storage solutions. This instance is highly suited for multi-threaded workloads, high-throughput web serving, database operations, and large-scale parallel processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.48xlarge is a compute-optimized server powered by the ARM64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. It features 192 dedicated physical cores and 192 vCPUs running at 2.7 GHz, paired with 384.0 GB of system memory. The instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware but offers a baseline network bandwidth of 300 Gbps. In benchmark testing, the c8gn.48xlarge delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and exceptional throughput for database operations and static web serving. While single-core performance remains average, its massive parallel processing capabilities and high network throughput make it highly cost-efficient for resource-dense deployments. It is best suited for multi-threaded applications, high-volume database operations, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.48xlarge is a compute-optimized ARM64 server featuring 192 dedicated physical cores, 384 GB of RAM, and 300 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.

tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized server featuring 192 dedicated physical cores, high memory bandwidth, and 300 Gbps baseline network performance.

384.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core.
High-speed networking with 300 Gbps baseline bandwidth.
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth in benchmark testing.
Optimized for high-throughput database operations, static web serving, and parallel processing.
